title: hibiscus manihot var. tetraphyllus
intro: Hibiscus manihot var. tetraphyllus, also known as the Yellow Bristleflower or Wild Okra, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its yellow or orange flowers and bristly foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Hibiscus manihot var. tetraphyllus include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting immune function, making it a popular remedy for various health conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
